period-archean-5838d159c8

File:The World Before There was Life by Knight.jpg

Nearly two billion years ago, volcanic activity was widespread, but the surface of the earth had cooled enough so that oceans could form. In this scene, life has not yet begun. Text above mural: Our infant world was barren and lifeless; volcanic activity reached a peak early in the history of the earth, 2 billion years

period-proterozoic-88edaa622c

File:Earliest records of earth life by Knight.jpg

Earliest records of earth life found in Proterozoic era deposited sediments one and one half billion years ago. Fossils are believed to be lime secretions of minute single celled plants, similar to some of the algae that live in hot springs today. Pre-cambrian or Precambrian; Charles Knight painting or mural installed

File:Ecology of Cambrian stage 3.jpg

Reconstruction of an ecological network in the Cambrian stage 3, around 521 Ma. Stigmaria is a form genus for tree roots of Carboniferous coal forest Lycopod trees such as Sigilaria and Lepidodendron. Carboniferous. period-jurassic-7ca81c3027

File:Life reconstruction of Berthasaura leopoldinae gen. et sp. nov. in the paleoenvironment represented by the “Cemitério dos Pterossauros” Quarry.jpg

The recognition of ontogenetic edentulism in the Jurassic noasaurid Limusaurus inextricabilis shed new light on the dietary diversity within Ceratosauria, a stem lineage of non-avian theropod dinosaurs known for peculiar craniomandibular adaptations. Until now, edentulism in Ceratosauria was exclusive to adult individu

period-jurassic-3447b5f563

File:A section of the Morrison Mural, which illustrates the Late Jurassic period. (9cd7c875-1dd8-b71b-0be1-e5b85a800692).jpg

This section of the Morrison Mural shows what the area may have looked like 150 million years ago. The Morrison Mural shows what we know about the Morrison environment, based on years of research by paleontologists and geologists. This is just a small section of the mural depicting plant and animal life as we think it

National Park Service
授权
Public Domain

period-eocene-2cc936d856

File:Adapis parisiensis Life Reconstruction.png

Life Reconstruction of adapid primate Adapis parisiensis climbing between branches of Eotrigonobalanus. Adapis is based on crania and jaws figured by Grandidier 1865 and Stehlin 1912, with postcranial proportions based on those calculated by Dagosto 1983, Europolemur and Darwinius.

File:Mammoth fossil tooth - Pleistocene, USA - 23202566565.jpg

Mammuthus sp. - fossil mammoth tooth from the Pleistocene of America. This is the occlusal surface (= chewing surface) of a molar from a mammoth - a type of extinct elephant. Mammoths were common in North America during the Pleistocene Ice Age. They are descended from Old World mammoths that crossed into North America

File:Pahoehoe lava (Blue Dragon Flow, Holocene, 2.030-2.076 ka; Craters of the Moon Lava Field, Idaho, USA) 8.jpg

Pahoehoe lava in the Holocene of Idaho, USA. Southern Idaho’s Snake River Plain represents the track of the Yellowstone Hotspot. Yellowstone is currently located in northwestern Wyoming. In the geologic past, the center of Yellowstone volcanism was in southern Idaho, and before that, it was located in southeastern Oreg

period-hunter-gatherer-0485f2ed01

File:Hunter gatherer's camp at Irish National Heritage Park - geograph.org.uk - 1252699.jpg

Hunter gatherer's camp at Irish National Heritage Park Exhibit showing how a 7000 B.C. campsite of Mesolithic period hunter gatherers would have looked. They were nomadic and built temporary houses. Wood, bone and flint were the materials of their tools. They fished using dugout canoes - there is one in the photo. More

period-first-civilizations-19ff784661

File:Urfa museum Uruk period wall mosaic technique - Chalcolithic 4959.jpg

The 4th millennium BC saw clay nails that were fired to be used in temples and other monumental structures representing the magnificence of the Uruk culture, used as mosaic like wall decorations in the following periods. The clay nails, that had conical shaped sharp tips, were inserted into wet and thick clay surfaces

period-first-civilizations-fb9a88939a

File:Clay tablet, record of barley and emmer. Late Uruk period, 3300-3100 BCE. Purchased via Christie's, no provenance.jpg

Clay tablet, late Uruk period, 3300-3100 BCE. Emmer wheat is differentiated from barley by writing numbers with extra strokes. The proto-cuneiform signs document barley. Barley appears 4 times here and is represented by a single stalk with ears at the top. Three different types of numerical symbol are used. Purchased v

period-first-civilizations-5e826f15d4

File:Sumerian cuneiform tablet dating approximately 2041 B.C. Is a receipt for the donation of livestock to the feast for a Sumerian king.jpg

The oldest piece in the McDonald Rare Book Collection at OSU is a Sumerian cuneiform tablet dating approximately 2041 B.C. The fragile piece has a prosaic purpose, it's a receipt for the donation of livestock to the feast for a Sumerian king. Date: Oct. 26, 2011 (photo: Theresa Hogue)

period-classical-civilizations-aedfca2368

File:View of the historical seat of the Metropolis of Corinth in the Roman Forum of Ancient Corinth on 6 April 2019.jpg

During Paul's stay in Corinth, he was brought for judgment before the proconsul Gallio on the accusation of conducting illegal teaching. Gallio, however, refused to judge what he considered to be a mere religious dispute among the Jews. According to tradition, the site of Paul's trial was the Bema, a large elevated ros

George E. Koronaios

period-classical-civilizations-1246833101

File:The Parthenon in Athens - DPLA - ac9fcf712832d2d593cad84c469741de.jpg

"The remains of some of the sculpture of Phidias under the peak of the roof. Most of them were carried off by Lord Elgin. This gives but a small idea of its beauty, none at all of its size. It is like all of these ruins, of beautiful white marble, not much darkened even by its extreme age, 500 B.C." albumen prints Imag

period-early-modern-510b7f7858

File:World Map illustrating the Portuguese Discoveries in the New World.jpg

Oval-projection world map illustrating the discoveries of the early Portuguese and Spanish explorers. The routes of Da Gama, Cabral, and Magellan are specifically noted. It does represent an unusual fusion of early 18th-century geography with the discoveries of the 15th- and 16th-centuries.

作者
Joseph-François Lafitau
授权
Public Domain
尺寸

A world map showing the voyages and routes of exploration of Europeans, including dates of ‘discoveries.’ The map is color-coded to show the territories explored and claimed by the Spanish in orange, and by the Portuguese in green.；From Duruy's General History of the World (vol. II)

creature-stromatolite-builders-3636ee7305

File:Chemosynthetic and Photosynthetic Microbial Mat.jpg

At East Diamante volcano (190 m, 623 ft depth), the chemosynthetic and photosynthetic communities overlap. Patches of encrusting red and green algae lie beneath filamentous bacterial mats on rock surfaces. The small white corals on the rocks are about 5 cm (2 in) long.

作者
Image courtesy of Submarine Ring of Fire 2004 Exploration, NOAA Vents Program
授权
Public Domain
尺寸

creature-stromatolite-builders-f492a81f7f

File:Chemosynthetic Microbial Mats.jpg

Chemosynthetic microbial mats cover red algae and coral, which are photosynthetic. Hydrothermal vent and coral reef communities are overlapping here at 190 m depth, something none of the scientists on the Submarine Ring of Fire Expedition have witnessed before.

作者
Image courtesy of Submarine Ring of Fire 2004 Exploration, NOAA Vents Program.

File:Stromatolite-encrusted fossil wood (Eocene; Blue Forest locality, Wyoming, USA) 9.jpg

Stromatolite-encrusted fossil wood from the Eocene of Wyoming, USA. (rough surface) "Petrified wood" refers to fossil wood that has had its microscopic porosity filled with minerals as groundwater percolated through, while buried in sediments. This fossilization process is technically known as permineralization ("petri

creature-cyanobacteria-24562645f5

File:Morania fragmenta (fossil bacteria) (Wheeler Formation, Middle Cambrian; Millard County, Utah, USA) 2.jpg

Morania fragmenta Walcott, 1919 - fossil bacteria from the Cambrian of Utah, USA. Click on the photo to zoom in - the dark gray and black splotches on this bedding plane are soft-bodied fossils interpreted as sheets of cyanobacteria, which are photosynthesizing microbes. File:Cyanobacteria 029.jpg

Bloom of cyanobacteria ("blue-green algae"). ； traces shaped streaks are traces of fish and ducks that run on water, "breaking" the biofilm. Conceptual model of potential interactions between iron reducers, methanogens, and anaerobic methanotrophic (ANME) archaea. File:SRM methanogen model results.png

Results of a kinetic reaction path model of sulfate reduction and methanogenesis. creature-charnia-61de470074

File:Charnia NT.jpg

Life restoration of Charnia masoni, a frond-like rangeomorph lifeform around 15 cm (6 in) long from the Ediacaran biota. The relationship of rangeomorphs like Charnia to animals and other kingdoms of life is unclear; one proposed grouping puts them in their own animal phylum Petalonamae. Charnia was first discovered in

(YPM 35467, Yale University's Peabody Museum, New Haven, Connecticut, USA) (centimeter scale) Soft-bodied macroscopic fossils have long been known from upper Neoproterozoic (Ediacaran) rocks, but they continue to generate much ex

creature-kimberella-e459a3a3a3

File:Kimberella diorama.jpg

This diorama of the Ediacaran animal Kimberella quadrata shows two K. quadratas (pink blobs on the right) scraping the surface of a microbial mat on the bottom of the sea floor. On the left of the Kimberellas, there are multiple burrows or traces of movement which are common fossils when it comes to Ediacaran fossils,

Animated reconstruction of Cooksonia pertoni. The stems have been reconstructed as non-photosynthetic, according to Boyce (2008). The axes are imagined to be around 100μm in diameter. creature-dimetrodon-3e0a45ed0a

File:Dimetrodon grandis in "high walk" pose.png

Reconstruction of the iconic Permian carnivore, Dimetrodon. It was known from a floodplain/wetland environment with periodic wet and dry seasons. As such, this coloration is ideal for a large-bodied, possibly scaly predator that may have needed to camouflage in varying conditions. The sail shows characteristic exposed

PrehistoryByLiam
授权

creature-dimetrodon-7578109552

File:Dimetrodon grandis 3D Model Reconstruction.png

A modern reconstruction of Dimetrodon grandis. The integument is based on evidence from Dimetropus and Estemmenosuchus showing it may have had scaleless skin with rectangular scutes on the underside of the belly. The upright posture is also based on the Dimetropus trackways, which shows Dimetrodon and closely related s

creature-coelophysis-8f4d2b6698

File:Coelophysis bauri mount.jpg

Coelophysis bauri (Cope, 1887) theropod dinosaur from the Triassic of New Mexico, USA. This is a remarkable complete skeleton of the small early theropod Coelophysis. It comes from a nearly monospecific concentration of numerous complete to disarticulated skeletons in reddish-colored fluvial siltstones, often called a

creature-ankylosaurus-253f6962c3

File:Ankylosaurus armor.png

Hypothesis of osteoderm morphologies and placements in Ankylosaurus magniventris. We suggest that the cervical armour was united into more typical half rings, and provide an updated osteoderm arrangement based on preserved osteoderms in AMNH 5214, AMNH 5895, and AMNH 5866, and comparisons with related species.

creature-velociraptor-33da7b4a7d

File:Velociraptor TD.png

Velociraptor mongoliensis is a diminutive dromaeosaur from the Late Cretaceous of what is now Mongolia. Smaller than most other dromaeosaurids, Velociraptor mongoliensis was about 2 m long and weighed around 18 kg in body mass. It was a bipedal, feathered carnivore with a long tail and an enlarged sickle-shaped claw on

creature-spinosaurus-0a4dfe698d

File:Spinosaurus aegyptiacus skeletal.jpg

Skull and skeleton of Spinosaurus aegyptiacus. A Skull reconstruction in left lateral view (after Dal Sasso et al. 2005) B Skull reconstruction with adult rostrum (MSNM V4047) and dentary (NHMUK VP R 16421) superimposed C Skeletal reconstruction (swimming pose, after Ibrahim et al. 2014b). Scale bars equal 40 cm in A,

Marco Auditore, published by Nizar Ibrahim, Paul C. Sereno, David J. Varricchio, David M. Martill, Didier B. Dutheil, David M. Unwin, Lahssen Baidder, Hans C. E. Larsson, Samir Zouhri, Abdelhadi Kaoukaya

creature-parasaurolophus-e90f2e10ac

File:Parasaurolophus walkeri, a new genus and species of trachodont dinosaur - PLATE I.png

Parasaurolophus walkeri. Skeleton as found, with neck abnormally flexed and head sheared downwards. The centra of the vertebrae have been drawn in from general measurements. About 1/24 natural size.

creature-quetzalcoatlus-4f6f9ad65b

File:Life restoration of a group of giant azhdarchids, Quetzalcoatlus northropi, foraging on a Cretaceous fern prairie.png

Life restoration of a group of giant azhdarchids, Quetzalcoatlus northropi, foraging on a Cretaceous fern prairie. A juvenile titanosaur has been caught by one pterosaur, while the others stalk through the scrub in search of small vertebrates and other food.

Mark Witton and Darren Naish

creature-mosasaurus-5553a4497b

File:Mosasaurus 21copy.jpg

Mosasaurus hoffmanni. Sources for anatomy: ~1:7 head-to-body ratio matches Fanti et al. (2014)[1] Shape of tail is based on soft tissue from the related Prognathodon per Lindgren et al. (2013)[2] The feeding on a turtle is based on fossil evidence per Lingham-Soliar (1995)[3]

Dmitry Bogdanov

creature-smilodon-1f25eb2d41

File:Smilodon fatalis endocast (LACMHC 2001-199).png

An endocast of a Smilodon fatalis (LACMHC 2001-199) estimated to 250kg. The specimen is 11.7cm, with a volume of 216ml, and a surface area of 256.51cm². The olfactory bulb area is 16.45 cm², with a neocortex of 120.3 cm². The neocorticalization of the specimen was calculated to 50.11%, with an encephalization quotient

An endocast of an Aenocyon dirus (LACMHC 2300-82) estimated to 80kg. The specimen is 11.7cm, with a volume of 181ml, and a surface area of 207.83cm². This image shows a 2.1 billion year old rock containing black-banded ironstone. The rock weighs about 8.5 tons, and is approximately two meters high, three meters wide, and one meter thick. It was found in North America and belongs to the National Museum of Mineralogy and Geology, Dresden, Germany. The rock is located

event-great-oxidation-f510e9f826

File:Pilbara, NW Australia (ASTER).jpg

The Pilbara in northwestern Australia exposes some of the oldest rocks on Earth, over 3.6 billion years old. The iron-rich rocks formed before the presence of atmospheric oxygen, and life itself. Found upon these rocks are 3.45 billion-year-old fossil stromatolites, colonies of microbial cyanobacteria. The image is a c

event-great-oxidation-820077bda2

File:Shark Bay Australia (MODIS 2018-07-13).jpg

Swirls of bright blue in the waters of Shark Bay, Australia point to the presence of phytoplankton in the bay. These plant-like microscopic organisms exist in the waters year-round, but reproduce in large numbers in when nutrients, daylight length, and water temperatures are favorable. At such times, the blooms become

The Burgess Shale Formation is one of the world’s most famous fossil fields, noted for its incredible preservation of the soft parts of fossils, dating from the Cambrian (535 million years ago). event-cambrian-explosion-f2a549c31c

File:Ecology of Cambrian stage 3.jpg

Reconstruction of an ecological network in the Cambrian stage 3, around 521 Ma. Suspension-feeding and simple trophic interactions are indicated with white arrows, habitat modification by grazing and scavenging with yellow arrows, and aquatic bioturbation by blue arrows. Reconstruction by Franz Anthony. Full reference

Eusthenopteron foordi Whiteaves, 1881 - fossil fish from the Devonian of Canada. The oldest known fish bones are fragments from the Upper Cambrian. Упрощённая филогения, охватывающая переход от рыб к четвероногим и показывающая произошедшие в его ходе основные анатомические изменения. Обратите внимание на существенное увеличение грудной клетки и таза. Рёбра присутствуют у эустеноптерона, но настолько малы, что их трудно увидеть. Таксоны расположены от наиболее при

Paleozoic diamictites near Porto, Portugal. The Valongo Anticline allows the observation of evolution of life during the Paleozoic, based on animal and plant fossils, as well as the possibility of dating the strata from the fossils they contain. Shales in the Devonian of Ohio, USA. This is a formation contact section in central Ohio. The unit in the upper part of the photo has relatively hard, dark-colored, chippy-weathering, marine mudshales of the basal Ohio Shale (Upper Devonian).
